_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 10-18 SPRN Death During Transport (MDHHS Reviewed 2022-12-27). MCA Optional — SPRN program participation required.
protocol_reference: Michigan Special Operations Protocol Section 10-18: SPRN Death During Transport — MDHHS Reviewed 2022
purpose: Define EMS procedures when a SPRN patient dies or suffers cardiac arrest during transport, including cessation of interventions, body containment, and coordination with CHECC and state authorities.

sections.title: Cardiac Arrest During SPRN Transport
content: IF SPRN PATIENT SUFFERS CARDIAC ARREST DURING TRANSPORT:
1. DO NOT initiate resuscitation interventions
2. STOP TRANSPORT — safely pull over at nearest appropriate location
3. Contact CHECC (Community Health Emergency Coordination Center) immediately
4. Contact online medical control
5. Await instructions from CHECC and MDHHS before any further action

RATIONALE: Resuscitation interventions (CPR, intubation, defibrillation) markedly increase aerosolization risk and crew exposure to HCID-contaminated body fluids. The risk to the transport crew is unacceptably high with confirmed HCID patient.

EXCEPTION: If patient had a pre-established RESUSCITATION STATUS CONFIRMED before transport (rare in SPRN context): contact online MC before initiating; proceed only with MC authorization and maximum PPE

sections.title: Body Containment — Bio Seal
content: After confirmed death (or cessation of resuscitation per CHECC/MC direction):
— DO NOT remove patient from MITD if MITD is in use — Bio Seal the device
— Bio Seal: apply biological containment seals (provided in MITD kit) to all access ports and zipper
— If patient is NOT in MITD: place patient in Category A biohazard-compatible body bag
BODY BAGS:
— Use TWO body bags (inner and outer) — double-bag
— Inner bag: sealed and labeled; outer bag: sealed and labeled UN 2814
— Label: decedent identifier (if known), date, 'CAUTION — CATEGORY A BIOHAZARD — HCID'
— Body remains double-bagged for all subsequent handling until cremation or designated disposition

sections.title: CHECC Coordination
content: CHECC: Community Health Emergency Coordination Center — state-level coordination center
Contact CHECC immediately upon SPRN patient death or cardiac arrest
CHECC provides:
— Authorization to stop transport and declare death
— Body disposition instructions — HCID decedents cannot go to standard funeral homes or mortuaries
— Coordinates with MDHHS, medical examiner, and public health for body release chain of custody
— Mortuary support: CHECC maintains a list of licensed crematoria approved to handle Category A HCID remains
— Provides guidance on law enforcement notification and death investigation
Do NOT contact local medical examiner independently — CHECC coordinates all HCID death notifications

sections.title: Body Disposition
content: HCID decedent body disposition requires MDHHS/CHECC authorization:
— Standard funeral home or mortuary services: NOT APPROPRIATE for confirmed HCID decedent — risk to mortuary personnel
— CREMATION at CHECC-approved facility: preferred disposition for confirmed HCID decedent
— CHECC maintains a list of approved crematoria equipped for Category A biohazardous remains
— Traditional burial: not recommended for confirmed HCID — requires special casket and burial protocols
— Family notification: MDHHS/CHECC coordinates with next of kin regarding HCID-specific body disposition requirements
Ambulance: remains out of service with body until CHECC provides body removal instructions; continue Protocol 10-15 decontamination after body removal

sections.title: EMS Team Procedures After Death
content: Remain at stopped vehicle location until CHECC provides instructions — do NOT proceed to hospital or transport body without CHECC direction
Maintain full SPRN PPE until body is removed and decontamination of vehicle is authorized
Contact agency supervisor and SPRN coordinator in addition to CHECC
Document: time of cardiac arrest/death, contact times and numbers called, CHECC instructions received
Crew health monitoring: heightened post-exposure monitoring given confirmed patient death; notify MDHHS medical officer
Critical incident debriefing: all SPRN team members involved in patient death should receive CISD (critical incident stress debriefing) — coordinate through agency

mca_selections_required.label: SPRN Program Participation — MCA authorization for SPRN protocols 10-10 through 10-18
mca_selections_required.description: MCA Optional: All SPRN protocols apply only to SPRN-designated agencies.

deterioration_flags: SPRN patient cardiac arrest — STOP; no resuscitation; contact CHECC immediately
Unable to reach CHECC — contact MDHHS SPRN coordinator (backup); contact online MC; do NOT proceed without guidance
scope_notes: MCA Optional — SPRN only. Resuscitation is NOT performed for SPRN patient cardiac arrest due to extreme crew exposure risk. Contact CHECC immediately — CHECC coordinates body disposition through MDHHS-approved crematoria. Standard funeral homes cannot accept HCID remains. All team members involved in SPRN patient death should receive CISD.
